The bioactive constituents of Pericarpium include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Pericarpium is often prepared into teas, decoctions, or tinctures, and is used to treat conditions such as hypertension, fever, and gastrointestinal disorders.
